events.speaker: operator
events.text: The owner designated one repository to hold the canonical application and a generalized public account of its process, governed by the root blueprint.

events.speaker: operator
events.text: The owner required literal placeholder redaction, generalized operator and assistant content, duplicate and archive removal, and separate build and cleanup visibility.

events.speaker: operator
events.text: The owner requested repository access, required the authoritative blueprint to govern the work, rejected a separate repository or private sibling edition, and approved two isolated workstreams within one repository.

events.speaker: system
events.text: Session instructions established repository, safety, tool, and parallel-track boundaries for the product work.

events.speaker: assistant
events.text: The product track inspected the repository, acknowledged the owner decisions, presented the same-repository plan, and started the application worktree.

events.speaker: tool
events.text: Repository, blueprint, branch, dependency, and scaffold checks established the product workspace and its initial test surface.

events.speaker: operator
events.text: The owner required autonomous execution, relayed cleanup-review findings, and repeatedly corrected status narration that did not yet establish the requested integrated result.

events.speaker: system
events.text: Continuation instructions kept the full product goal active and required evidence-based completion rather than a narrower checkpoint.

events.speaker: assistant
events.text: The product track implemented the React application, adopted the cleanup track's public-content interface, addressed review findings, and reported integrated verification progress.

events.speaker: tool
events.text: Code edits, content synchronization, image work, unit tests, browser checks, accessibility checks, builds, privacy checks, and branch integration produced and verified the first integrated product candidate.

events.speaker: operator
events.text: The owner challenged the elapsed time and incomplete handoff, asked whether the cleanup track was still waiting, and required the agent to act rather than merely acknowledge the missed coordination.

events.speaker: system
events.text: Session constraints continued to require the observable repository outcome and an honest distinction between local integration and publication.

events.speaker: assistant
events.text: The product track admitted the missed handoff, completed the integration signal, and clarified that the verified candidate was still not published on the default branch.

events.speaker: tool
events.text: Branch and integration checks confirmed the handoff state without changing remote history.

events.speaker: operator
events.text: The owner asked to view the application, tested it on mobile, questioned the missing historical wheel and passive use of supplied images, and requested review of the original image-prompt transcript.

events.speaker: system
events.text: The review turns constrained the assistant to inspect and answer before making further product changes.

events.speaker: assistant
events.text: The product track exposed a local preview, inspected the preserved wheel implementations and supplied images, then admitted that the application had treated purpose-built interface artwork as passive illustration.

events.speaker: tool
events.text: Local-server checks, source searches, visual inspection, and full transcript review established the wheel and image-composition fidelity gap.

events.speaker: operator
events.text: The owner clarified the requested review, asked whether the intended correction was feasible, checked source-image resolution, and requested a continuation plan for the product repair.

events.speaker: system
events.text: The session preserved inspection-only boundaries while the correction scope and next handoff were defined.

events.speaker: assistant
events.text: The product track confirmed that the correction was feasible, explained the available source assets, produced a continuation plan, and withheld publication because the known product gap would make the rewrite stale.

events.speaker: tool
events.text: Asset and transcript checks supplied the evidence used to define the next product correction pass.

events.speaker: operator
events.text: The owner defined the cleanup track, required the six-phase public record and literal redaction policy, and relayed independent-review defects that had to be fixed before rewriting history.

events.speaker: system
events.text: Initial session instructions established the cleanup objective, repository boundary, safe tool behavior, and completion standard.

events.speaker: assistant
events.text: The cleanup track accepted the scope, created the isolated history branch, generalized the existing sources, and responded to successive review findings while initially treating product-track messages as reasons to pause the rewrite.

events.speaker: tool
events.text: Repository inspection, canonicalization, transcript generation, duplicate and archive removal, audit receipt generation, and early regression runs produced the first cleanup checkpoints.

events.speaker: operator
events.text: The owner repeatedly restored focus to Track B, rejected unnecessary waiting, asked why the rewrite was paused, and restated the complete cleanup objective and coordination boundary.

events.speaker: system
events.text: Goal continuation and repository-safety instructions required the cleanup track to keep progressing without crossing into application ownership or claiming premature completion.

events.speaker: assistant
events.text: The cleanup track acknowledged the corrections, hardened redaction and provenance, fixed source-slot attribution and replacement counting, expanded removed-asset accounting, and prepared a safer rewrite workflow.

events.speaker: tool
events.text: Independent review, regression tests, source-range verification, binary and metadata checks, receipt regeneration, and repeated branch verification produced the pushed cleanup handoff.

events.speaker: operator
events.text: The owner supplied the integrated product state, authorized the local rewrite dry run, prohibited premature force-pushing, and asked for a plain estimate while final verification was running.

events.speaker: system
events.text: The active goal continued to require exact integrated verification and default-branch publication before repository-wide completion.

events.speaker: assistant
events.text: The cleanup track resumed the integrated rewrite, diagnosed topology, tiled-image, URL-scheme, regex-source, and neutral-fixture defects, then reported an exact-tree verified mirror and fresh-clone handoff.

events.speaker: tool
events.text: Test-first fixes, repeated ephemeral mirrors, all-history privacy checks, topology and timestamp comparison, exact tree comparison, and fresh-clone product and cleanup suites verified the integrated rewrite without changing remote refs.

events.speaker: operator
events.text: The owner challenged whether the cleanup track had truly finished, requested a plain-language account of the work, prohibited further messages to the product track, and required both Codex sessions to be added to the public process record.

events.speaker: system
events.text: The final session instructions kept the full repository goal active while preserving the Track B boundary and the prohibition on further product-track messaging.

events.speaker: assistant
events.text: The cleanup track distinguished its completed rewrite handoff from the unpublished repository-wide result, admitted earlier coordination errors, and accepted responsibility for extending the public transcript record through both Codex tracks.

events.speaker: tool
events.text: Goal, repository, branch, phase, provenance, and private session-source checks established the inputs for extending this public record without committing either raw session.

generalizationNotice: Every event is a concise generalized statement. Nothing in this transcript is presented as a verbatim quotation.
title: Codex build
